Hope this information is useful #iqpc Later, was part of the panel discussing the steps to be taken by organization, related to change management to increase the adoption of Agentic/Gen AI/ML solutions.
...
Over the past few years I have seen incredible progress in artificial intelligence. What is becoming clear now is that AI is not just changing analytics or data science. It is fundamentally changing how we design and build applications.
...
is starting to shift that
...
Applications are becoming much more proactive. Instead of just presenting information, they are increasingly able to identify opportunities, highlight risks, and recommend specific actions.
...
For organizations with field sales teams, supply chains, or complex operations, this shift can be powerful. Insights that used to take hours of analysis can now surface directly within the workflow where decisions are made.
...
In many cases the biggest opportunity is not replacing people with AI. It is augmenting people with better insights delivered at the right moment.
...
We are still early in this transformation, but it is exciting to see how AI is reshaping the way enterprise applications deliver value.
...
Curious to hear how others are seeing AI influence the way applications are designed and how insights are delivered to end users. #ArtificialIntelligence #DigitalTransformation #SalesTechnology

results.title: Rahul Shah
highlights:
Analytics Lead at [Campbell Soup Company](https://www.linkedin.com
...
soup-company)
...
Passionate and motivated professional, working towards a degree in Business Analytics at University of Illinois at Chicago (UIC), with experience and interest in Predictive Modeling, Statistical Analysis, Machine Learning, Data Analysis, Data Management, Data Visualization, Business Intelligence and Operations Research. I have gained extensive knowledge in theory and application of Big Data analytics and Deep Learning methods and techniques. In past, I graduated with a Master’s degree in Industrial Engineering at UIC, where I gained extensive experience in Statistical and Probabilistic Analysis, Stochastic Processes, Time Series Forecasting, Quality Control, Simulation, and Optimization. My competencies include: • Technical Skills: Classification, Regression, Exploratory Data Analysis, Data Cleaning/Transformation, Data Sampling, Dimensionality Reduction, PCA, Linear Optimization, Generalized Linear Models, Linear/Logistic Regression, LASSO, Ridge, Naïve Bayes, Linear/Quadratic Discriminant Analysis, KNN, Decision Trees, Random Forests, Gradient Boosting, AdaBoost, SVMs, Neural Networks, Deep Learning, Clustering, Association Rules, NLP, Databases • Languages: Python, R, SQL, Scala, MATLAB, SAS, C++, JAVA • Tools: Tableau, MS SQL Server, PostgreSQL, RapidMiner Studio, VBA, MS Excel, Word, Power Point Being able to think in a contemplative way, identify patterns and relationships about various issues of practical importance that are not obvious to others has perhaps become my greatest strength. I believe that my experience, aided by a solid multidisciplinary engineering and business background, would certainly help me face the industry as a well-equipped professional. Yet, I realize the need to widen my knowledge and technical expertise to deal with complex data in this data-driven world. Looking for: Data Scientist, Data Analyst, Business Analyst, Business Intelligence Analyst, Marketing Analyst, Financial Analyst, Credit Risk Analyst or related positions.

results.title: Rob Stewart
highlights:
Sr. Talent Acquisition Partner at Campbell's
...
Sr. Talent Acquisition Partner at [The Campbell's Company](https://www.linkedin.com/company/campbell-soup-company)
...
I'd like to believe I'm an organized and diligent individual, that takes pride in whatever it is that I do. I started my professional career coaching soccer. After 5 successful years of coaching both at the college and youth levels, where I focused heavily on player development and player recruitment, I transitioned into professional talent acquisitions. Currently, I focus on managing the hourly hiring for 3 plants in the Campbells company portfolio.l I have successfully taken my previous experience and transitioned to professional staffing & recruiting. I have worked every aspect of an agency desk, from New Business Development, Client Management, Candidate Sourcing, and handling the Recruitment process from beginning to end. I have successfully placed people in a multiple of fields throughout my career, in both temporary and permanent positions. A true generalist, some of those fields include Life Sciences, Biotechnology, Pharmaceutical, Chemical, Food & Beverage, Industrial, Architecture, Engineering, Sales, Legal, Administrative, Customer Service, and Clerical type positions. Also, I run the adult soccer programs (Men's & Women's) at Liederkranz Soccer Club in Mansfield, OH, as well as sit on their social board as a Trustee.. Prior to returning to the Liederkranz, where I began my adult playing career, I spent time with Lighthouse SC and United German Hungarians (both from the Philadelphia area), Akron GFS and Alderson Broaddus University.

# Data Scientist (GenAI and Machine Learning)
...
- Get In Touch
- Job Category: Data AI & ML Jobs
- Job Type: Remote
...
Job Category: Data AI & ML Jobs
...
### Responsibilities
...
- Develop and apply machine learning models, including techniques such as decision trees and forecasting, to solve business problems
- Use generative artificial intelligence methods to extract insights from complex document sets, including structured and unstructured text
- Perform data analysis, feature engineering, and model development using Python
- Collaborate with cross-functional teams to interpret data, communicate findings, and support decision-making
- Translate domain-specific language and requirements into actionable data science solutions
- Continuously refine models and analytical approaches to improve accuracy and performance
...
### Required experience and skills
...
- Strong background in data science, with hands-on experience in machine learning model development
- Proficiency in Python, including libraries such as Pandas for data manipulation and analysis
- Working knowledge of SQL for querying and managing data
- Experience with generative artificial intelligence, including prompt-based techniques
- Solid understanding of statistical methods and analytical techniques
- Experience with machine learning approaches such as tree-based models and forecasting methods
- Ability to communicate complex analytical concepts clearly to both technical and non-technical stakeholders

highlights:
Data Scientist (100%) - MDPI Switzerland
...
Product & Technology · Basel Office - MDPI Headquarters
...
# Data Scientist (100%)
...
## Drive innovation in machine learning and AI, from research to deployment, creating scalable intelligent systems that power the next generation of scientific discovery.
...
Are you passionate about turning complex data into intelligent, production-ready solutions while supporting the future of open-access science? We are looking for a Data Scientist to join our team and help design, develop, and deploy advanced machine learning solutions that power intelligent, data-driven products. This role focuses on NLP, recommender systems, and agentic LLM workflows, turning complex business challenges into scalable and impactful analytical solutions.
...
This is an opportunity to work at the forefront of machine learning and AI, contributing to innovative systems that leverage state-of-the-art research in practical applications.
...
Core Responsibilities
...
Design, develop, and evaluate machine learning and statistical models, with a focus on NLP, recommender systems, and LLM-based solutions.
...
Translate business problems into data-driven approaches and scalable analytical solutions.
...
Perform data exploration, preprocessing, and feature engineering to ensure high-quality inputs for modeling.
...
Develop, optimize, and benchmark models using appropriate metrics and validation strategies.
...
Build and experiment with LLM-powered systems, including agentic workflows and RAG.
...
Investigate, reproduce, and compare research prototypes and state-of-the-art methods.
...
Collaborate with engineering teams to support the integration of models into production.
...
Monitor and analyze model performance and iterate to improve accuracy and robustness.
...
Bachelor’s degree/ Master's degree in Computer Science or related.
...
2-5 years of experience as a Data Scientist.
...
2–5 years of experience in Python (including complex applications), Machine Learning, and LLMs.
...
Strong background in data science, statistics, and analytical problem-solving.
...
Intermediate proficiency in FastAPI, Celery, and Keycloak.
...
Intermediate proficiency in PyTorch, TensorFlow, Scikit-learn, and Hugging Face.
...
Proficiency in Natural Language Processing (NLP), including tokenization and named entity recognition (NER).
...
In-depth understanding of Artificial Intelligence principles.
...
Strong working knowledge of Microsoft O365 tools.
...

results.title: MMAI Kiersten Campbell
highlights:
GTLP @ Telus | MMAI | Aspiring AI Leader
...
Artificial Intelligence is my passion and my long-term goal is to lead groundbreaking AI projects with a real impact! I firmly believe that (responsible) AI is the future, and that we have only scratched the surface of what is possible with technologies like Chat-GPT. During my Computer Science degree, I immersed myself in the world of AI taking coursework in machine learning and the neurology of learning. My thesis on Responsible AI was the crowning achievement and passion project of my undergraduate journey. Currently, I'm a Master of Artificial Intelligence Management (MMAI) student at the Schulich School of Business in Toronto. As an MMAI I have the ability to translate technical concepts to non-technical people while understanding the unique business needs of the tech industry. My intensive work as a stage-manager taught me that I have a natural skill for leadership, project-management paperwork and organizing projects. My experiences in the startup space have fueled my passion for creating innovative, ground-breaking technology solutions in AI. I know AI can be a confusing and broad field so let me clear up what I can do! Although I am a generalist, my true forte lies in understanding AI applications from the ground up, not merely in adapting existing LLM APIs to web applications or engineering prompts for chat-GPT. I have hands-on experience in designing and developing AI solutions from scratch, giving me a profound understanding of the intricacies involved in creating cutting-edge AI systems. If you're looking for someone who can bridge the gap between business and technical teams or identify future business strategies in AI projects, I bring expertise in a wide range of areas. From Computer Vision (Dall-E, facial recognition, self-driving, object detection) and NLP (Chat-GPT, Google Translate) to Predictive Modelling (predicting future trends), Automation (automating manual tasks), Classification (identifying customer churn, spam detection), and Neural Networks (Google search and complex/high-dimensional predictions), I have you covered. As I build my career path, I anticipate becoming an integral part of a team where I can leverage my skills to drive technical business outcomes and continue my growth as a leader. For me, company culture is of paramount importance, and I am confident in my ability to make a substantial contribution to an organization deeply invested in the future of AI, fostering an environment that nurtures my growth as a leader. *Written by leveraging OpenAI's Chat-GPT. Edited by a human.

results.title: Staff AI Engineer - AI Creator | Careerport
highlights:
AI Engineer - AI Creator

Remote, USA

Posted 2026-06-11

Full-time

 About DataCamp DataCamp's mission is to empower everyone with the data and AI skills essential for 21st-century success. By providing practical, engaging learning experiences, DataCamp equips learners and organizations of all sizes to harness the power of data and AI. As a trusted partner to over 17 million learners and 6,000+ companies, including 80% of the Fortune 1000, DataCamp is leading the charge in addressing the critical data and AI skills shortage. About the role Research has consistently shown that one-on-one tutoring dramatically outperforms traditional classroom instruction—a finding known as Bloom's 2 Sigma Problem. Despite decades of awareness, no solution has successfully delivered personalized tutoring at scale. Until now. We’re building an AI Tutor that feels less like software and more like a world-class personal coach—guiding millions of learners in real time, adapting to their goals, and unlocking faster, deeper understanding at scale.. You will work on the core AI system that delivers real-time, adaptive learning experiences to learners. If you're excited about building the future of human learning, this role is for you. About you You’re a strong builder who thrives on turning cutting-edge AI capabilities into real, high-impact products. You operate with high ownership, move quickly, and care deeply about quality—balancing experimentation with disciplined execution. You’re comfortable working in ambiguity but don’t wait for perfect direction—you break down problems, make progress, and iterate. You want to work on systems that matter, alongside people who are pushing the boundaries of how AI can transform learning. Responsibilities Own the design and implementation of key components of the AI tutoring system, including prompt architectures, agentic workflows, and adaptive behaviors. Drive experiments end-to-end—shipping, measuring, and iterating to improve learning outcomes and model performance. Build production-grade systems that integrate LLM capabilities into user-facing experiences with reliability, speed, and scalability. Translate product and pedagogical goals into concrete technical solutions, working closely with product, content, and design. Rapidly prototype and deploy new model capabilities, bridging the gap between research breakthroughs and real user impact. Strengthen system quality through robust testing, monitoring, and iteration—ensuring safe and consistent behavior at scale. Raise the bar through strong code, pragmatic technical decisions, and active participation in design and code reviews. Qualifications Technical Proven experience building and shipping LLM-powered products in production environments. Strong prompt engineering skills and hands-on experience with agentic workflows or multi-step model interactions. Solid grasp of experimentation—comfortable designing, running, and interpreting experiments to drive improvements. Strong software engineering fundamentals (Python), with experience building scalable systems and working in modern production environments. Product & Pedagogy Strong product instincts—able to connect model behavior to user experience and outcomes. Interest in building effective learning experiences; curiosity about how people learn and where AI can accelerate that. Ability to work closely with non-engineering stakeholders to ship cohesive, user-centered solutions. Character & Collaboration High ownership and bias for action—you move things forward without waiting for perfect clarity. Comfortable operating in fast-moving environments with evolving constraints. Clear communicator who contributes to a strong, low-ego engineering culture. Why Datacamp?  Joining DataCamp means becoming part of a dynamic, creative, and international start-up. Here are just a few of the reasons why you’ll love being on our team: Exciting challenges: Face new technical challenges daily,

highlights:
# Sr Quality Process Engineer
...
Since 1869 we've connected people through food they love. We’re proud to be stewards of amazing brands that people trust. Our portfolio includes the iconic Campbell’s brand, as well as Michael Angelo’s, Pace, Pacific Foods, Prego, Rao’s Homemade, Swanson, and V8. In our Snacks division, we have brands like Cape Cod, Goldfish, Kettle Brand, Lance, Late July, Pepperidge Farm, Snack Factory, and Snyder’s of Hanover. Here, you will make a difference every day. You will be supported to build a rewarding career with opportunities to grow, innovate and inspire. Make history with us. Why Campbell’s… Benefits begin on day one and include medical, dental, short and long-term disability, AD&D, and life insurance (for individual, families, and domestic partners). Employees are eligible for our matching 401(k) plan and can enroll on the first day of employment with immediate vesting. Campbell’s offers unlimited sick time along with paid time off and holiday pay. If in WHQ – free access to the fitness center. Access to on-site day care (operated by Bright Horizons) and company store. Giving back to the communities where our employees work and live is very important to Campbell’s. Our “Campbell’s Cares” program matches employee donations and/or volunteer activity up to $1,500 annually. Campbell’s has a variety of Employee Resource Groups (ERGs) to support employees.
...
The Quality Engineer is a Technical Service position that focuses on loss areas of opportunity – driven by consumer dissatisfaction or financial losses delivering enablers and improved consumer experience. The role will be responsible for assessing standards for Critical to Process parameters, identifying areas of variation and developing action plans to address variation and drive consistency of product design on Critical to Quality parameters. This role will focus on improving compliance and driving problem solving in the identified loss area with a systematic approach. This position designs, leads and executes projects of various scale. These projects may be geared toward food safety, quality, productivity or other areas of need.

### Intelligent generation and management of estimates for application of updates to a computing device
...
The present disclosure is directed to automated generation and management of update estimates relative to application of an update to a computing device. One or more updated to be applied to a computing device are identified. A trained artificial intelligence (AI) model is applied that is adapted to generate an update estimate predicting an amount of time that is required to apply an update to the computing device. An update estimate is generated based on a contextual analysis that evaluates one or more of: parameters associated with the update; device characteristics of the computing device to be updated; a state of current user activity on the computing device; historical predictions relating to prior update estimates for one or more computing devices (e.g., that comprise the computing device); or a combination thereof. A notification of the update estimate is then automatically generated and caused to be rendered. Show less
...
### Computing system for training, deploying, executing, and updating machine learning models
...
A server computing device generates training data based upon an identifier for a device, a timestamp, and a label received from a developer computing device. The server computing device trains a computer-implemented machine learning (ML) model based upon the training data. The server computing device also generates client configuration data for the ML model that specifies transformations that are to be applied to values in order to generate input values for the ML model. The server computing device deploys ML assets to client computing devices, the ML assets comprising the ML model and the client configuration data. The client computing devices execute the ML model using input values derived via transformations of (local) values produced by the client computing devices and transmit telemetry data to the server computing device. The server computing device updates the ML assets based upon the telemetry data. Show less
...
